release_judgment: LinguaSpindle v0.1.0 is ready for a WSL2/Linux Technical Preview release.

native_windows.detail: Native Windows execution: Not in scope for this supplemental acceptance.

git.detail: The first audited, buildable, and tested v0.1.0 baseline commit was created after staged-file and exact-secret scans passed.

results.detail: Ordinary /usr/bin/docker resolves to the Docker Desktop WSL Linux CLI and reaches Client/Server 29.6.1 in the default context.

The current workspace-write, managed/restricted Codex sandbox resolves and runs the Linux client but Engine-backed commands fail with permission denied at unix:///var/run/docker.sock; the same CLI passes outside the sandbox, so this is not a WSL integration failure.
Ordinary WSL Linux docker shows Client and Linux Server 29.6.1, Docker Desktop 4.82.0, and linux/x86_64 Engine info without invoking Windows docker.exe.
Two auth.docker.io OAuth requests timed out.
AWS Public ECR Docker Official Image mirror seeded python:3.12-slim digest sha256:57cd7c3a7a273101a6485ba99423ee568157882804b1124b4dd04266317710de; original no-cache Dockerfile build then passed.
Compose parses, starts, becomes healthy, and reports application/database ok at version 0.1.0; ordinary Linux docker compose restart and post-restart ps/health also pass.
UID/GID 10001, privileged=false, read-only root, no-new-privileges, one rw /data Volume, no Docker socket.
Web assets and SQL migration are installed; no model/font asset suffixes found below /app.
Same Project, Job, Steps, logs, Segments, Artifact IDs, and TXT SHA-256 after compose restart.
Container/network removed and recreated without -v; named Volume and business data retained.
Image rebuild and force-recreate retained data and Artifact bytes.
WSL Chromium 149.0.7827.55 targeted the Docker GUI; Mock and opt-in real Provider flows passed.
Create/upload/run/poll/Steps/logs/results/QA/TXT/JSON/failure flow passed.
Single image, translated/raw Artifacts, CBZ download and member validation passed.

A three-Segment novel Job used the Docker GUI, ApplicationService, durable Job runner, and DeepSeek OpenAI-compatible endpoint; all six Steps, QA, TXT/JSON exports, usage capture, and secret scans passed.
No service URL/models/fonts/license/runtime conditions; GUI and Job show unavailable and ADAPTER_UNAVAILABLE.
800-Segment Job recovered from SIGKILL as PROCESS_INTERRUPTED; explicit retry reused 3 upstream Steps and 28 succeeded Segments, then produced 800 unique successful Segments.

Post-call scans covered 43 Docker /data files (3019776 bytes) and 33 browser/evidence/report files (9092891 bytes), with zero exact-secret or Authorization/Bearer hits; no identity schema or Provider secret column exists.
Job Artifacts had Step provenance; disposable Project cleanup removed metadata and payload and returned 404 afterwards.
Ruff format/check, strict mypy, compileall, 69 default tests, 82% coverage, Mock Docker browser, and opt-in real Provider browser passed.
Optional check unsupported because the slim image has no ps executable.
Only actual host and container versions were required.

real_provider.limitations: Long documents, concurrency, rate limits, and long-term stability were not validated.

codex_sandbox.error: permission denied while trying to connect to the docker API at unix:///var/run/docker.sock

docker_revalidation.classification: Codex sandbox/session environment issue, not a WSL Docker integration issue; Windows docker.exe is not required.

code_changes.detail: The existing Playwright framework gained an explicit paid-test opt-in and existing-Job evidence mode; Provider results capture standard token usage in redacted Step logs; Compose now forwards its existing timeout/concurrency/retry settings; public repository metadata points to taoning0403/lingua-spindle; the Typer CLI now supports the required --version release check. Schema and API contracts did not change.

blocked_items: Current Codex agent sandbox access to /var/run/docker.sock (ordinary WSL Docker passes)
